title: Responsive Web Design Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential techniques and philosophies for building mobile-friendly websites.

questions.question: What is 'Responsive Web Design'?
options: A way to create a completely different site for mobile users.
A web design approach where a single site responds to the user's screen size and device characteristics.
An automated tool that converts HTML into a mobile app.
A CSS library used specifically for animations.

questions.question: Which meta tag is required in the <head> to ensure browsers correctly scale a responsive web page?
options: <meta name='responsive' content='yes'>
<meta name='viewport' content='width=device-width, initial-scale=1.0'>
<meta name='scale' content='auto'>
<meta charset='UTF-8'>

questions.question: The 'Three Pillars' of Responsive Design include fluid grids, media queries, and which of the following?
options: Static layouts
Flexible images
High-definition fonts
Fixed-width tables

questions.question: In Responsive Design, what is a 'breakpoint'?
options: A specific point in the HTML code where a script stops running.
A screen width where the layout of the page changes via a media query.
A section of a grid where content is forced to break to a new line.
A link that leads to a mobile-only version of the page.

questions.question: What is the primary benefit of a 'Mobile-First' design strategy?
options: It focuses only on users who use mobile phones.
It prioritizes essential content and ensures performance on lower-powered devices first.
It eliminates the need for desktop versions of the site.
It automatically optimizes images for high-resolution screens.
